title: NIS2 Vulnerability Disclosure Policy Operational Signals

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing: SPL directly addresses Art.21(2)(e) requirements with specific, actionable monitoring.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIS2 Art.21(2)(e) (Security in acquisition, development and maintenance) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.2.27: NIS2 Vulnerability Disclosure Policy Operational Signals.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.2.27' running on index=itsm (vuln disclosure tickets), index=email_gateway (parsed security@), HEC from bug bounty APIs, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

dataSources: `index=itsm` (vuln disclosure tickets), `index=email_gateway` (parsed security@), HEC from bug bounty APIs
app: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928), HTTP Event Collector (HEC)
spl:
index=itsm sourcetype="snow:incident" category="*Vulnerability Disclosure*" earliest=-90d
| eval opened=strptime(opened_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval triaged=strptime(work_start,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval triage_hours=round((triaged-opened)/3600,2)
| eval sla_breach=if(triage_hours>48 OR isnull(triaged),1,0)
| stats count as tickets sum(sla_breach) as breaches avg(triage_hours) as avg_triage
| eval breach_pct=round(100*breaches/tickets,1)
| table tickets, breaches, breach_pct, avg_triage
description: Coordinated vulnerability disclosure and cooperation with researchers support systemic resilience. This use case tracks intake of `security@` reports, HackerOne/Bugcrowd submissions, and PGP-encrypted mailbox events — measuring time-to-triage against internal policy.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIS2 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes email_gateway, itsm and sourcetypes snow:incident. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Publish disclosure policy URL in security.txt and monitor referrers optionally; (2) auto-create ServiceNow incidents from email and bounty webhooks; (3) alert on SLA breaches; (4) link findings to engineering Jira; (5) annual report on researcher cooperation metrics. Indexes required: `index=email_gateway`, `index=itsm`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=snow:incident`.
visualization: Table (breached tickets), Line chart (triage time trend), Single value (breach %), Bar chart (by product team).
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities by Vulnerabilities.dest, Vulnerabilities.severity | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Reports may queue during major incidents while details are confirmed, or during initial 24h triage.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928), HTTP Event Collector (HEC).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=itsm` (vuln disclosure tickets), `index=email_gateway` (parsed security@), HEC from bug bounty APIs.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Publish disclosure policy URL in security.txt and monitor referrers optionally; (2) auto-create ServiceNow incidents from email and bounty webhooks; (3) alert on SLA breaches; (4) link findings to engineering Jira; (5) annual report on researcher cooperation metrics.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype="snow:incident" category="*Vulnerability Disclosure*" earliest=-90d
| eval opened=strptime(opened_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval triaged=strptime(work_start,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval triage_hours=round((triaged-opened)/3600,2)
| eval sla_breach=if(triage_hours>48 OR isnull(triaged),1,0)
| stats count as tickets sum(sla_breach) as breaches avg(triage_hours) as avg_triage
| eval breach_pct=round(100*breaches/tickets,1)
| table tickets, breaches, breach_pct, avg_triage
```

Understanding this SPL

**NIS2 Vulnerability Disclosure Policy Operational Signals (Art. 21(2)(e))** — Coordinated vulnerability disclosure and cooperation with researchers support systemic resilience. This use case tracks intake of `security@` reports, HackerOne/Bugcrowd submissions, and PGP-encrypted mailbox events — measuring time-to-triage against internal policy.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=itsm` (vuln disclosure tickets), `index=email_gateway` (parsed security@), HEC from bug bounty APIs. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928), HTTP Event Collector (HEC). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: itsm; **sourcetype**: snow:incident. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=itsm, sourcetype="snow:incident", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **opened** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **triaged** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **triage_hours** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **sla_breach** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `stats` aggregates the pipeline (counts, distinct values, sums, percentiles, etc.) into fewer rows.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **breach_pct** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.

This use case does not use a CIM tstats shortcut; the primary SPL above is the source of truth after field normalization in your environment.

Output fields: avg_triage, breach_pct, breaches, opened, sla_breach, tickets, triage_hours, triaged.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare ticket and field counts with the ServiceNow ITSM or GRC console (same time range) and confirm synchronization fields and sourcetypes used by this search.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (breached tickets), Line chart (triage time trend), Single value (breach %), Bar chart (by product team).
grandmaExplanation: We coordinated vulnerability disclosure and cooperation with researchers support systemic resilience.
